D4 Colorimetry L5 Evaluate Reference

Scale the Whole, or Clip Each?

take a full ring of saturated hues and watch global scaling keep its circular shape while per-colour clipping warps it to the gamut

Faced with out-of-gamut colours you can clip each one independently to the nearest edge, or scale every colour by one shared factor so the most saturated just fits. Clipping preserves in-gamut colours but distorts relationships; scaling preserves relationships but desaturates everything.

A ring of hues sits at one chroma (faint = source). Switch between clip and scale. Clipping snaps the ring onto the lumpy gamut boundary; scaling shrinks it to a smaller, still-circular ring that fits entirely inside.
